title: Cassia standleyi
taxonomy: Cassia standleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subgenus Bactis, which consists of tropical plants native to Central and South America. The subgenus Bactis is part of the genus Cassia, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as senna. The family Fabaceae is a large and widespread group of plants, commonly known as legumes. Cassia standleyi is closely related to other species within the subgenus Bactis, sharing similar characteristics and growth habits.

Further research is necessary to confirm the effectiveness of Cassia standleyi's medicinal uses.
